food_subgroup: Stir-fried instant noodle dish with chicken

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Maggi Goreng with Chicken. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Noodles, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Malaysian stir-fried instant noodles with chicken.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific prepared noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Maggi Goreng with Chicken as typically served at a Malaysian hawker stall or restaurant, approximately 350 g.

nutrient_source.title: Mee goreng (fried noodles) — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / adapted with chicken component from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD; FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD mee goreng (fried noodles) entry and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for chicken-containing stir-fried noodle dishes. Maggi Goreng with Chicken is a stir-fried instant noodle dish closely analogous to mee goreng. Values represent a composite estimate per 100 g of the prepared dish including chicken, egg, vegetables, and seasoning. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients are reported as -1 where not available in the source.

gi_source.title: Instant noodles, cooked — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Maggi Goreng with Chicken as a composite dish. GI of 47 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked instant noodles (GI approximately 47, low category), which forms the primary carbohydrate base of this dish. Stir-frying with protein (chicken), egg, vegetables, and fat is expected to lower the effective GI further relative to plain boiled noodles. GL per serving calculated as: (47 × 20.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 350 g serving) / 100 = approximately 34 g. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from instant noodle seasoning and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Maggi Goreng with Chicken due to its high sodium content (approximately 480 mg per 100 g, equating to roughly 1680 mg per standard 350 g serving), which represents a substantial portion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load with low GI from instant noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: Maggi Goreng with Chicken has a low glycemic index (approximately 47) due to the dense starch structure of instant noodles, but the glycemic load per serving is high (approximately 34 g) owing to the large portion size; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat from stir-frying with cooking oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content and moderate total fat from stir-frying in cooking oil make Maggi Goreng with Chicken a dish that individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume infrequently, preferring versions prepared with less oil and reduced-sodium seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Goreng with Chicken (Malay: Maggi Goreng Ayam) is a popular Malaysian stir-fried instant noodle dish made by wok-frying Maggi brand instant noodles with chicken pieces, egg, onion, tomato, chilli, and soy-based seasoning. The dish is a hawker staple across Malaysia and Singapore, commonly served at mamak stalls and kopitiams. The noodles are first boiled briefly, then tossed in a hot wok with oil, aromatics, and the chicken, producing a dish with a slightly charred, smoky flavour (wok hei), a chewy noodle texture, and a savoury-spicy taste profile. Nutritionally, Maggi Goreng with Chicken provides moderate energy (approximately 175 kcal per 100 g), meaningful protein from chicken and egg (approximately 8.5 g per 100 g), and carbohydrate from the instant noodles (approximately 20.5 g per 100 g). The dish is notable for its high sodium content due to the instant noodle seasoning packet and soy sauce, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is low (approximately 47) due to the dense starch structure of instant noodles, though the glycemic load per standard serving is substantial. The dish is typically halal and dairy-free but contains gluten, egg, and soy allergens. It is widely available at Malaysian and Singaporean hawker centres, mamak restaurants, and food delivery platforms.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Maggi Goreng chicken?
What is the GI of Maggi Goreng?

What are the ingredients in Maggi Goreng with chicken?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD mee goreng (fried noodles) data and FAO/INFOODS SEA references, as no exact Maggi Goreng with Chicken entry exists in a primary approved source. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked instant noodles. Confidence is medium because the dish composition (chicken quantity, oil volume, seasoning) varies by vendor and preparation style.
